multiplechoiceresponse.label: In Lecture 2's fairness-aware optimization, why is the binary variable \(Z_i\) introduced?

choicegroup.choice: To reweight observations from underrepresented groups
To remove biased samples from the dataset
To decide which outcome labels should be flipped to achieve demographic parity
To directly modify protected attributes such as race or gender

div.p: The variable \(Z_i\) is included so the optimization can choose which labels to flip in order to reduce disparities between groups. The protected attributes themselves are not modified, and observations are not removed or reweighted. Instead, fairness is achieved by altering a limited number of labels in a controlled way.
